name: Fried Empanadas with Beef or Chicken Filling
recipeInstructions:
Dough: Oil or a mixture of oil and solid vegetable shortening for deep frying.
To make dough: Sift the flour, add the melted shortening and egg. Add salted water gradually and mix until a firm dough forms. Knead by hand for 10 minutes, using an electric mixer with a dough hook for 4-5 minutes, or process in a food processor with a dough hook for 1-2 minutes. Let rest 15 to 30 minutes.
Beef filling: Heat oil and shortening in a frying pan. Sauté green onions until lightly browned. Add meat and cook until done. Stir in tomato puree and remaining ingredients, cooking briefly longer. Allow to cool completely before assembling empanadas.
Chicken filling: Heat oil and sauté onions until soft. Add capsicum and cook until tender. Incorporate minced chicken and stir until fully colored. Season as desired, add tomato puree, and simmer for 3 minutes. Let cool completely before assembling empanadas.
Assembly and cooking: Roll dough to about 3 mm thick. Cut into circles of about 14 cm diameter using a saucer or similar guide. Divide filling evenly onto one side of each circle. Moisten the outer rim with water and fold into a semi-circle. Fold ends slightly to form a crescent shape. Seal edges by folding them over in small sections, forming a rope effect. If confusing, press together and decorate with a fork as usual. Heat oil for deep frying (about 6-8 cm depth) until very hot. Fry empanadas one or two at a time until golden brown. Remove from oil immediately and drain on paper towels. Serve hot.
Halve the filling ingredients or double the dough if making both beef and chicken empanadas. Consider adding raisins or sultanas for flavor contrast with the meat.
